title: War on Iran: Netanyahu's Political Survival vs. National Security
content:
The ongoing military conflict in the Middle East has increasingly blurred the lines between national security and domestic political survival. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu astonished both political enemies and allies by holding onto power after the bloodiest day in his nation's history. Despite maintaining a fractious coalition, opinion polls showed that public support for his leadership remained stubbornly low for years. Consequently, many analysts suspect that Netanyahu's recent political conduct has been heavily driven by his desire for personal political survival. There are legitimate concerns that, at times, his immediate political considerations may outweigh the country's long-term national security interests.

This dynamic is particularly evident in the escalating tensions and military exchanges with Iran and its regional proxies. Some high-profile critics argue that the war was expanded due to internal political pressure rather than imminent external threats. If this sentiment gains broader traction globally, it could severely damage Israel's most important diplomatic relationships. Furthermore, despite the aggressive military campaign, the expected domestic political boost for the ruling coalition has not materialized. Public opinion researchers note that there has been no significant rally in trust for the government, with approval ratings quickly declining to prewar levels.
